title: Anthropic Introduces Claude Opus 4 and Sonnet 4, Setting New AI Coding Standard
summary: Anthropic launched Claude Opus 4 and Sonnet 4, its most capable models yet, with Opus 4 claiming the top spot on software engineering benchmarks and the ability to work autonomously for hours on complex agent tasks.
content: Anthropic has released Claude Opus 4 and Claude Sonnet 4, a new generation of hybrid reasoning models that switch between near-instant responses and extended thinking. Claude Opus 4 tops industry coding benchmarks with a 72.5% score on SWE-bench Verified and can sustain complex agentic workflows for several hours without losing focus — a capability real-world partners like Rakuten have validated with 7-hour autonomous refactoring runs. Claude Sonnet 4 scores 72.7% on SWE-bench and is positioned as a fast, cost-efficient upgrade from Sonnet 3.7, with GitHub Copilot adopting it as the engine for its new coding agent. Both models support extended thinking with parallel tool use and significantly improved memory — when given local file access, Opus 4 proactively creates memory files to maintain continuity across long tasks. Claude Code is now generally available with VS Code and JetBrains integrations, background task execution via GitHub Actions, and a new extensible SDK for building custom agents.

title: OpenAI Launches Codex, Its Most Capable AI Coding Agent, in ChatGPT
summary: OpenAI released Codex, a cloud-based software engineering agent built on the o3 reasoning model, capable of autonomously writing features, fixing bugs, and running tests across multiple parallel tasks in sandboxed GitHub-connected environments.
content: OpenAI has launched Codex in research preview — its most capable AI coding agent yet — now available to ChatGPT Pro, Enterprise, and Team subscribers. Codex runs on codex-1, a fine-tuned variant of the o3 reasoning model trained via reinforcement learning on real-world coding tasks; it produces cleaner code than o3, adheres more precisely to instructions, and iteratively runs tests until they pass. Each task executes in its own sandboxed cloud container preloaded with the user's GitHub repositories, and the agent can run multiple tasks in parallel without interrupting the user's workflow. Jobs typically take one to 30 minutes and include writing features, fixing bugs, answering codebase questions, and drafting pull requests for review. OpenAI simultaneously released a smaller codex-mini model as the new default for its open-source Codex CLI terminal agent, available in the API at $1.50 per million input tokens.

title: Google DeepMind's AlphaEvolve Discovers Better Algorithms, Recovers 0.7% of Global Compute
summary: Google DeepMind unveiled AlphaEvolve, an evolutionary AI agent that uses Gemini models to iteratively discover and optimize algorithms, delivering measurable efficiency gains across Google's data centers, chip design, and Gemini training itself.
content: Google DeepMind has announced AlphaEvolve, an evolutionary coding agent that pairs Gemini Flash and Gemini Pro models with automated evaluators to propose, score, and refine algorithmic solutions as runnable code. In production at Google for over a year, AlphaEvolve's data center scheduling heuristic continuously recovers an average of 0.7% of Google's worldwide compute — a massive saving at Google's scale — while remaining fully human-readable and debuggable. The system also sped up a critical matrix multiplication kernel in Gemini's architecture by 23%, reducing total Gemini training time by 1%, and achieved a 32.5% speedup for the FlashAttention kernel used across transformer-based AI models. In mathematical research, AlphaEvolve was tested against over 50 open problems in geometry, combinatorics, and number theory — matching state-of-the-art solutions 75% of the time and finding improved solutions in 20% of cases, including a new lower bound for the 300-year-old kissing number problem in 11 dimensions. Google DeepMind is planning an early access program for academic researchers and exploring broader availability.

title: US House Passes 10-Year Moratorium on State AI Regulation in Budget Bill
summary: The US House of Representatives voted 215-214 to embed a 10-year ban on states enforcing AI laws in its budget reconciliation bill, representing the most significant federal move yet to preempt the rapidly growing patchwork of state AI regulation.
content: On May 22, 2025, the US House of Representatives passed a budget reconciliation bill containing a provision that bars states and localities from enforcing any laws or regulations governing AI models, AI systems, or automated decision systems for a full decade. The measure passed by the slimmest possible margin — 215 to 214 — along strict party lines, with Republicans arguing it prevents a confusing compliance landscape while Democrats contend that states have been forced to act because Congress has not. The moratorium's scope is broad enough to block enforcement of state laws addressing deepfakes, online safety, age verification, facial recognition by law enforcement, and algorithmic discrimination in hiring — with only narrow exceptions for laws that carry criminal penalties or actively facilitate AI deployment. More than 1,000 AI-related bills have been introduced across US states in just the first five months of 2025. The bill now moves to the Senate, where it faces procedural challenges under the Byrd Rule, and Senator Ted Cruz has separately announced plans to introduce a standalone 10-year moratorium bill through the Senate Commerce Committee.

title: Google Rolls Out AI Mode to All US Users, Signals Total Reinvention of Search
summary: At Google I/O 2025, Google announced AI Mode — its Gemini-powered conversational search experience — is now available to all US users, with new agentic features including Deep Search, autonomous web browsing via Project Mariner, and real-time camera-based Search Live.
content: Google announced at I/O 2025 that AI Mode, its Gemini-powered chatbot tab inside Search, is rolling out to all users in the United States, moving from a limited experiment to a broadly available feature. Beyond answering questions, AI Mode can now conduct Deep Search — turning a single prompt into many parallel searches and synthesizing the results — and includes Project Mariner, an agent that can autonomously browse the web and complete real-world tasks like booking travel or purchasing concert tickets. Search Live lets users have a real-time voice conversation with Search while pointing their camera at whatever they're investigating. AI Mode can also access users' historical search queries and, optionally, Gmail, to personalize responses based on individual context. Google reported that 1.5 billion people now see AI Overviews in their search results monthly, with the company's search leadership stating the product will be unrecognizable compared to today within three years as it shifts from 'information retrieval' to 'intelligence.'

title: Google Upgrades Gemini 2.5 with Deep Think Reasoning Mode and Improved Flash Model
summary: At Google I/O, Google unveiled Deep Think — an experimental enhanced reasoning mode for Gemini 2.5 Pro that considers multiple hypotheses simultaneously — alongside a faster, more efficient Gemini 2.5 Flash and new agentic capabilities including computer use and native MCP support.
content: Google announced substantial upgrades to its Gemini 2.5 model family at I/O 2025, headlined by Deep Think, an experimental enhanced reasoning mode for Gemini 2.5 Pro that uses new research techniques to consider multiple hypotheses before committing to an answer. Deep Think scores highly on the 2025 USAMO math competition benchmark, leads the LiveCodeBench competition coding leaderboard, and scores 84.0% on the MMMU multimodal reasoning benchmark. An updated Gemini 2.5 Flash, now available to all Gemini app users, delivers improved reasoning, code, and long-context performance while using 20-30% fewer tokens than its predecessor — with general availability in Vertex AI planned for early June. New capabilities across both models include native audio output for real-time conversational voice experiences, Project Mariner's computer use API for agents that can control browsers and apps, native Model Context Protocol (MCP) support for integrating open-source tools, and strengthened defenses against indirect prompt injection attacks. Thinking budgets — previously only available for Flash — are being extended to Gemini 2.5 Pro, giving developers direct control over how many tokens the model uses to reason before responding.
